page: Amazon Web Services i3.xlarge is a storage-optimized, third-generation virtual server powered by the Xen hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. This configuration provides 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, supported by a 45 MB L3 cache. The system includes 30.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 7.62 GB of RAM per core.

A defining characteristic of this instance is its bundled local storage, consisting of a 950 GB NVMe SSD. It does not include any GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The hardware profile is tailored for high-speed local data access, though the network throughput and older CPU architecture present clear bottlenecks for distributed or highly network-dependent workloads.

Benchmark results indicate that the compute and memory subsystems operate in the lower performance tiers. Single-core and multi-core CPU tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, consistently register in the bottom 10% to 25% of modern cloud hardware. Memory bandwidth is similarly constrained, with large-block write speeds falling into the bottom 10%. However, the presence of a fast local NVMe SSD makes this instance qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring high-density, low-latency local storage without demanding state-of-the-art CPU performance.

The i3.xlarge is best suited for storage-intensive applications that benefit from direct NVMe access, such as NoSQL databases, transactional databases, and local caching layers. It is also appropriate for moderate web serving and Redis deployments where storage capacity and read/write speeds are prioritized over raw computational throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services i3.xlarge is a storage-optimized virtual server utilizing the Xen hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor offering 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a 2.3 GHz clock speed. The instance features 30.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage, while lacking GPU acceleration and providing a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place its compute and memory capabilities in the lower tiers, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores in the bottom 10% to 25%. Consequently, the i3.xlarge represents a qualitative trade-off, sacrificing raw CPU power for dense, low-latency local storage. It is best suited for storage-centric workloads such as NoSQL databases, transactional databases, and local caching systems.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3.xlarge is a storage-optimized virtual server featuring 4 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD, designed for database and caching workloads requiring dense local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i3.xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized virtual server featuring dense local NVMe SSD storage and balanced memory for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 4 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
Bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency data access
30.0 GB of system memory providing 7.62 GB of RAM per core
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps on a Xen hypervisor
Best suited for NoSQL databases, transactional databases, and caching layers
